_comment: v6 hotspots for FGFR2 selective binder design — derived from paired-PISA + mCSM-PPI2 + multi-paralog MSA

hotspots.rationale: Largest single-residue energetic contributor to FGF1 binding. Carboxylate forms salt-bridge anchor with FGF1 K12 backbone (PISA-validated). Paralog-conserved (FGFR1 D282 also engages, BSA 24.9 — symmetric, no selectivity). Drop this and you're not competing with FGF1's strongest contact.

Largest interface BSA in entire pocket. Guanidinium two-arm grip: H-bonds FGF1 H93 backbone AND N95 sidechain simultaneously (PISA-validated, double anchor). Paralog-conserved (FGFR1 R250 BSA 122.8). Non-negotiable for affinity.

THE BEST RESIDUE in our analysis. mCSM top-5 affinity AND CRAC asymmetry (paired-PISA): FGFR2 buries 49 A^2 of FGF1 surface, FGFR1's Y280 buries only 3 A^2. Same residue identity, different docking geometry — the binder inherits the asymmetry. Dual purpose in one slot.
mCSM rank 3 affinity contributor. D3 βF/βG strand region (rigid secondary structure → high pAE confidence in AlphaFold → high ipSAE).

STRONGEST single-residue selectivity signal in entire interface. PISA: V317 buries 127 A^2 of FGF1 in FGFR2; FGFR1's V316 buries ZERO. FGFR1 has nothing at this position to confuse a binder. Modest mCSM (-0.41) is not the point — V317's value is geometric paralog discrimination, not affinity. Drop this and selectivity collapses to N173 alone.

Only chemistry-based selectivity hotspot that survived paired-PISA. FGFR2 has Asn (neutral H-bond donor/acceptor); FGFR1 has Lys (positively charged) at the equivalent position 172. A binder side chain that H-bonds N173's amide will electrostatically clash with FGFR1's K172. Compounded selectivity with V317.

rejected_hotspots.reason: Paired-PISA refuted mCSM signal. FGFR1 K164 has BSA 7.5 vs FGFR2 R165 at 4.5 — FGFR1 actually engages MORE. Targeting R165 would not differentiate paralogs.
Actively harmful. FGFR1 P283 has BSA 36.3 vs FGFR2 A284 at 21.8. A binder targeting A284 would prefer FGFR1. Eliminate.
Not in either paralog's interface (BSA = 0 in both 1DJS and 1EVT). Geometric ghost. Was 6.7 A from FGF1 — rim, not contact.

evidence_sources: PISA (per-residue BSA on 1DJS chain A vs 1EVT chain C, paired comparison)
mCSM-PPI2 (empirical alanine scan ΔΔG, trained on SKEMPI mutation database)
Multi-paralog MSA (FGFR1/2/3/4 from UniProt, BLOSUM62 + star alignment)
PeSTo (independent ML binding-site prediction on FGFR2 alone)
Plotnikov 2000 Cell — original 1DJS paper, validates affinity-vs-selectivity decoupling
Cao 2022 Nature — only published de novo FGFR binder (mb7); targets D3 backside, NOT FGF1 site (different problem)

ipsae_maximization_notes.why_six_not_more: IPD documentation states 3-6 hotspots is the empirical sweet spot. More hotspots dilute the interface signal and reduce ipSAE because the TM-score-like normalization sums over interface residues — adding moderate-confidence residues lowers the average.
ipsae_maximization_notes.why_atom_level_in_RFD3: RFD3 supports atom-level select_hotspots; specifying carboxylate oxygens (D283: OD1,OD2) vs default CA increases AlphaFold's confidence in the H-bond geometry, which directly increases ipSAE.
ipsae_maximization_notes.why_HardTarget_in_BindCraft: HardTarget prediction protocol uses initial-guess complex prediction. Per IPD this increases complex confidence ~10-15% over default. Single biggest BindCraft lever for ipSAE.
